medications.indication_rationale: First-line fungicidal treatment for tinea corporis; apply to the lesions and about 2 cm beyond the active border, continuing roughly 1-2 weeks past clearance.
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply topically twice daily to and about 2 cm beyond the border (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Night-time itch relief to restore sleep while the antifungal takes effect.
medications.dose_route_frequency: 10 mg orally once daily (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Drives tinea incognito by suppressing local immunity — must be stopped, not continued or re-prescribed onto the lesions.
Reserved for extensive, refractory or markedly steroid-modified disease not clearing with topical therapy.
No secondary bacterial infection is present (infection screen negative); antibiotics are an error of commission unless the safety-net triggers.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Stop steroid creams on the lesions, apply the antifungal about 2 cm beyond each ring's edge, wash hands after touching the kitten or applying cream, do not share towels or clothing, and have the kitten vet-checked.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Halts the spread, prevents household transmission, and removes the animal reservoir causing reinfection.

patient_education_points.point: This is ringworm — a common fungal infection, likely picked up from your new kitten. The cortisone cream fed it and made it spread, so stop using that. The antifungal cream kills the fungus — apply it a couple of centimetres past the red ring.
Keep applying the antifungal for about a week after the skin looks clear, wash your hands and don't share towels, and get the kitten checked by a vet to stop it coming back.
If a ring becomes hot, hard or swollen, oozes thick yellow pus, or you develop a fever, seek medical care promptly.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Resolution of the lesions; consider a short oral antifungal if not clearing.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Signs of secondary infection (hot/hard swelling, thick pus, or fever) — seek care sooner.

must_include_items: Recognise tinea corporis (annular scaly advancing border with central clearing) and STOP the topical steroid (tinea incognito)
Topical antifungal applied to and beyond the active border, continued past clearance
Hygiene / no towel-sharing advice and a vet check of the animal source
Secondary-infection safety-net (± short oral antifungal if extensive or refractory)
acceptable_alternatives: An alternative topical azole antifungal (e.g. clotrimazole or miconazole)
A short oral antifungal for extensive or refractory disease
errors_of_omission: Not treating beyond the active border
errors_of_commission: Prescribing or continuing a topical steroid on the lesions
Systemic antibiotics or ED referral in the absence of secondary infection
Over-triaging benign ringworm to urgent in-person assessment
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: A pass requires antifungal treatment (extended past the border) plus stopping the steroid plus source/hygiene advice, with a secondary-infection safety-net. A correct self-care and routine-review answer PASSES; escalation to urgent in-person care for this benign presentation is an over-triage defect.
